_README._comment: Documentation block, NOT a zone. Keys beginning with '_' are ignored by every consumer: zone-manager and rules-manager skip them on load, and the jq-based bash tooling selects only entries with a state/vlantag. Do not add network fields here.

isolation_invariant.rule: Tier-4 isolation zones (iotCams, iotUntrust) are defined with 'access-to': [] and MUST NEVER appear in any zone's 'access-to'. Inbound access is granted exclusively per-module, by listing the source zone in the target's 'pinhole-allowed-from' and declaring the specific VM-IP-to-port rule in that module's install.sh.
isolation_invariant.mgmt_exception: mgmt (Tier-0 control plane) is the sole exception: it lists every standard service/client/IoT/DMZ zone, including the Tier-4 zones, in its 'access-to' for full operational visibility. No other zone may.
isolation_invariant.rationale: Adding an isolation zone to a non-mgmt 'access-to' nullifies the pinhole mechanism: every host in the source subnet would gain unconditional, zone-wide reach regardless of any module declaration. iotCams must stay purpose-limited (GDPR Art. 25 - privacy by design); iotUntrust must stay quarantined to prevent lateral movement on compromise.
isolation_invariant.review_note: Three working-tree additions violating this invariant (iotUntrust added to srvHome/home access-to; srvCust added to work access-to) were caught and reverted pre-commit. With the rule written here, a reviewer can distinguish an accidental violation from an intentional design change.

access_mechanisms.access-to: Baseline reachability set by the zone designer in this file. Grants the ENTIRE source subnet access to the ENTIRE target subnet.
access_mechanisms.pinhole-allowed-from: Lists the zones that MAY open per-module pinholes into this zone. The actual narrow rule (specific source VM IP -> specific port) is declared at install time by the module's install.sh, not here.

tier_model: Tier 0 - Control plane: mgmt. Egress: all zones. Ingress: no inbound pinholes.
Tier 1 - Service backends: srvHome, srvWork, srvCust, srvDev, srvTest, dmz. Egress: internet (+ declared IoT). Ingress: module pinholes via pinhole-allowed-from.
Tier 2 - Trusted clients: home, work. Egress: internet + own service zone. Ingress: direct.
Tier 3 - IoT controlled: iotLocal, iotCloud. Egress: iotCloud=internet, iotLocal=none. Ingress: zone-wide from srvHome/home via access-to.
Tier 4 - IoT isolated: iotCams, iotUntrust. Egress: iotCams=none, iotUntrust=internet. Ingress: pinhole-only, no exceptions (see isolation_invariant).
Tier 5 - Untrusted clients: guest. Egress: internet only. Ingress: no inbound pinholes.
_README.mdns_policy: Multicast service discovery (Sonos/SSDP, Chromecast) does not cross VLAN boundaries on its own. Where a trusted client or service zone must discover devices in iotCloud, run udpbroadcastrelay to forward UDP port 1900 (SSDP) between the relevant interfaces. Referenced by iotCloud._comment.

iot_classification._comment: Decision aid for placing a new IoT device into iotLocal / iotCloud / iotCams / iotUntrust. Referenced by iotUntrust._comment.
split_policy_3_questions: 1. Does the device require internet to function? No -> iotLocal (no egress). Yes -> continue.
2. Is the firmware/vendor trusted and kept updated? No -> iotUntrust (internet-only, quarantined). Yes -> continue.
3. Does it capture personal or sensitive data (e.g. cameras)? Yes -> iotCams (no egress, pinhole-only access). No -> iotCloud.

pr_review_checklist: No Tier-4 zone (iotCams, iotUntrust) appears in any non-mgmt zone's access-to.
Every new non-internet access-to entry is justified with a named use case in the PR.
Isolation-zone access is declared per-module in install.sh (pinhole-allowed-from), not via access-to here.
Every zone has both _comment and description; operational instructions live in _comment only.

mgmt._comment: Always-on control plane. Full access to all zones for operational control. No inbound pinholes — mgmt initiates only.
mgmt.description: Control plane: hypervisors, backup, firewall, identity, cicd

netbird._comment: NetBird WireGuard overlay (issue #367). NOT a VLAN/interface zone: state=Manual + vlantag=0 mean zone-manager never creates an interface, DHCP, or firewall rules for it (the 'bridge' field is inert, present only to satisfy the schema). It exists so network:proxy access-list.sh can resolve the NetBird peer CIDR and admit tunnel peers through Caddy: peers terminate on OPNsense's OWN interface, so the routing-peer masquerade does NOT rewrite their source — Caddy sees the peer's overlay IP (e.g. 100.70.x.x), which matched no 10.x zone and was 403'd. Per-peer authorization is enforced by NetBird's own access policies (only the Admins group gets the route to OPNsense), so allowing the whole overlay CIDR here is safe. The default 100.64.0.0/10 is NetBird's full CGNAT allocation range and covers any sub-range the management server hands out (e.g. 100.70.0.0/16); narrow it only if you deliberately restrict NetBird to a smaller network. Included in the default Caddy allow-set like mgmt; services with an explicit proxyAllowedZones must add 'netbird' to keep tunnel access.
netbird.description: NetBird VPN overlay: admin tunnel peers (not a VLAN)

edge._comment: ADR-010 satellite WireGuard infra tunnel (NOT a VLAN: state=Manual + vlantag=0, so zone-manager creates no interface/DHCP). The home (OPNsense) end dials OUT to the satellite's public IP; this /31 is the tunnel link. The satellite is a semi-trusted external relay and gets LEAST-PRIVILEGE, ROLE-GATED reach only: edge -> Caddy ingress (reverse-proxy), edge -> OPNsense admin-WG listener (admin-vpn), edge -> home PBS :8007 (backup) — and NOTHING else, never broad mgmt. Those specific-port rules are applied per active satellite role by satellite-manager; their exact zones-schema form is TBD (ADR-010 implementation Q6), so access-to/pinhole-allowed-from are empty here.
edge.description: ADR-010 satellite infra tunnel (external relay; least-privilege)

admin._comment: ADR-010 admin-vpn landing zone (Q3 RESOLVED, validated live 2026-07-01). The operator's admin WireGuard session TERMINATES on OPNsense (a dedicated 'tappaas-admin' WG server on port 51821, tunneladdress 10.255.1.1/24); the satellite only blind-relays the UDP (or the admin device connects direct to the cluster WAN — the OPNsense side is identical). access-to: ['mgmt'] is realised NOT by zone-manager (which skips Overlay/vlantag=0 zones) but by a single explicit least-privilege firewall pass rule on the 'wireguard' interface: 10.255.1.0/24 -> 10.0.0.0/24, created idempotently by 'satellite-manager admin setup' (lib/admin-vpn.sh). NetBird (the 'netbird' overlay) remains independent for many-peer mesh; admin-vpn covers the single-operator case with plain WireGuard and no control plane. Peers are managed with 'satellite-manager admin add-peer'. Full runbook: tappaas-cicd/ADMIN-VPN.md.
admin.description: ADR-010 admin-vpn landing (mgmt tunnel; see ADMIN-VPN.md)

srv._comment: Flat-topology alternative to srvHome/srvWork/srvCust. Use only when zone segmentation is not required. Set state=Active to enable.
srv.description: Generic service zone — single-zone deployments only

srvHome._comment: Personal-tier services: Home Assistant, household apps. home reaches zone-wide via its access-to; mgmt via its access-to.
srvHome.description: Personal services: home automation, personal apps

srvWork._comment: Business-tier services: internal apps, productivity stack. work reaches zone-wide via its access-to. Public exposure via dmz pinholes declared by hosted modules; mgmt via its access-to.
srvWork.description: Business services: internal apps, productivity stack

srvCust._comment: Customer and tenant sandboxes. No client zone has default access. Public exposure via dmz pinholes only. Tenants are mutually isolated by subnet.
srvCust.description: Customer/tenant sandboxes: mutually isolated, internet-only egress

srvDev._comment: Development environment. Combined dev+test for small teams (<10, no compliance requirement). For ISO 27001 A.8.31 or teams >10, use srvTest separately. No dmz pinhole — not publicly reachable.
srvDev.description: Development services: internal workloads, not public-facing

srvTest._comment: QA and acceptance test environment. Separate from srvDev to enforce dev/test isolation per ISO 27001 A.8.31. No client zone has default access. Set state=Active when a dedicated test phase is required.

home._comment: Trusted personal devices on the home network. Reaches srvHome and iotLocal/iotCloud zone-wide for consumer app compatibility (Hue, Shelly, Sonos). Does not reach srvWork — use the work zone for managed corporate access.
home.description: Trusted personal devices: laptops, phones, tablets

work._comment: Managed corporate devices. Reaches srvWork only. Set state=Disabled for pure-home deployments. For MSP/consultant deployments, srvCust access is opt-in via deployment config.
work.description: Managed corporate devices: laptops, phones, tablets

iot._comment: Flat-topology alternative to iotLocal/iotCloud/iotCams. Use only when IoT sub-zone segmentation is not required. Set state=Active to enable.
iot.description: Generic IoT zone — single-zone deployments only

iotLocal._comment: Local automation devices: no internet, no LAN egress. Zigbee/Hue/KNX/Shelly. srvHome and home reach zone-wide via access-to; srvHome modules may create VM-specific pinholes via pinhole-allowed-from. mgmt via its access-to. (= The Hook Up's 'NoT'.)
iotLocal.description: Local-only IoT: no internet, automation devices

iotCloud._comment: Cloud-connected trusted devices: smart appliances, media (Sonos, Chromecast), HVAC/solar monitoring. srvHome and home reach zone-wide via access-to. Sonos/SSDP needs udpbroadcastrelay port 1900 (see _README.mdns_policy).
iotCloud.description: Internet-dependent trusted IoT: smart appliances, media, monitoring

iotCams._comment: Cameras and NVR. No internet, no LAN egress. NVR module in srvHome or srvWork reaches via explicit pinhole only — never zone-wide access-to (GDPR: access is purpose-limited). Usually wired PoE; no SSID.

iotUntrust._comment: Untrusted internet-required devices (cheap gadgets, unknown firmware). Internet-only; no LAN access; no inbound pinholes. Isolated from iotCloud to prevent lateral movement on compromise. Set state=Active to enable. See _README.iot_classification.split_policy_3_questions.

guest._comment: Untrusted visitor devices. Internet-only; no service or device zone access; no inbound pinholes.

dmz._comment: Reverse proxy and internet-exposed services. Internet-only egress (ACME, upstream calls). Internet inbound via pinhole-allowed-from. Backends (srvWork, srvCust) expose themselves inbound via their own pinhole-allowed-from declarations.

test._comment: Generic network probe for basic connectivity validation. Activate to verify routing and internet egress from an isolated subnet. For inter-zone rule validation use testAllowA/testAllowB and testPinhole instead.
test.description: Generic test zone — basic connectivity probe (inactive)

testAllowA._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Bidirectional access with testAllowB and dmz validates inter-zone allow rules.
testAllowA.description: Test zone A: bidirectional allow validation (inactive)

testAllowB._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Bidirectional access with testAllowA and dmz validates inter-zone allow rules.
testAllowB.description: Test zone B: bidirectional allow validation (inactive)

testPinhole._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Only testAllowA in pinhole-allowed-from, not in access-to — validates auto-pinhole path (see issue #173).
